notes: A single transcript event is unusually large: pi flushes its rollout JSONL once per message at turn completion (no partial flushes; concern C8), so one prompt that elicits a LARGE assistant reply (e.g. 'print the numbers 1 to 400, each on its own line') yields ONE oversized assistant JSONL line. The tailer + line parser must ingest that line WITHOUT stalling and the turn boundary must survive, so the session still settles ready→working→ready with no stuck working. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE pi driver (tmux REPL): a single send + wait_turn keeps the rollout alive so the daemon observes the working→ready settle cleanly, then one tmux teardown yields a single process_exited (concern C6). C7 / SESSION-IDENTITY: the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch; session_birth asserts only 'a session appears in ready within 2s' and legitimately binds that pre-session with NO session pin. turn_start carries NO session pin so the matcher binds the FIRST working — necessarily the UUID-keyed rollout session, since the pre-session never goes working — and every later phase pins to it via same_session_as. ROBUSTNESS CLAIM: the 'oversized line' size property is NOT a phase-DSL form; the absence of a parser stall is INFERRED from this clean arc completing (no stuck working, no waiting). The genuine >64 MB cap behavior is covered by core/pkg/tailer/large_line_test.go (readLineCapped / bufio.ErrBufferFull handling, issue #270), not by this live recording — a live CLI cannot be coerced into a multi-megabyte single line, so this recording is a sanity-check that the arc completes on a large-single-line turn.

text: Session appears in ready within 2s of launch
User prompt eliciting a large single reply → working (binds the FIRST working = rollout session)
Oversized assistant line parsed without stalling → turn completes, back to ready; state never entered waiting (no parser stall causing stuck working)
PID bound to the rollout session
pi exits at REPL teardown — single process_exited on the rollout session
